source_document: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorism Financing) Act 2012
full_text: Every financial institution and cash dealer shall, as soon as practicable after forming a suspicion that a transaction or proposed transaction may be relevant to the investigation or prosecution of a person for a money laundering offence or a financing of terrorism offence, report the transaction or proposed transaction to the FIU.
summary: Financial institutions must report suspicious transactions to the FIU immediately upon forming suspicion of money laundering or terrorism financing.
penalty_range: Up to MUR 1,000,000 fine for failure to report

source_document: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorism Financing) Act 2012
full_text: Every financial institution shall report to the FIU every cash transaction or series of cash transactions in the same business day that exceeds the prescribed threshold.
summary: Mandatory reporting of cash transactions exceeding the prescribed threshold (USD 15,000 equivalent). No suspicion required — threshold alone triggers the report.

source_document: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorism Financing) Act 2012
full_text: Any person who imports or exports monetary instruments exceeding the prescribed amount shall make a declaration. Every financial institution facilitating such movement shall report to the FIU.
summary: Cross-border movement of monetary instruments exceeding USD 10,000 must be declared and reported to the FIU.
penalty_range: Up to MUR 1,000,000 fine and/or seizure

source_document: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorism Financing) Act 2012
full_text: A person shall not disclose to any other person that a report has been made under this Act, or that an investigation is being or may be carried out.
summary: Criminal offence to tip off a suspect or third party that an STR has been filed or an FRC investigation is underway.
penalty_range: Up to 5 years imprisonment and/or MUR 1,000,000 fine

source_document: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering and Terrorism Financing) Act 2012
full_text: Every financial institution shall keep all records of transactions and customer identification for a minimum period of 7 years.
summary: Financial institutions must retain transaction records and customer identification for at least 7 years.

source_document: Financial Intelligence and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2002
full_text: Every financial institution shall establish and maintain policies for customer identification and verification, including beneficial ownership, before entering into a business relationship.
summary: KYC is mandatory before establishing business relationships. Includes beneficial ownership verification.
penalty_range: Up to MUR 500,000 and possible licence revocation

source_document: Financial Intelligence and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2002
section_title: Enhanced due diligence for high-risk customers
full_text: Financial institutions shall apply enhanced customer due diligence in respect of Politically Exposed Persons and transactions involving high-risk jurisdictions.
summary: Enhanced due diligence (EDD) mandatory for PEPs and high-risk jurisdictions.

full_text: If a financial institution suspects that funds are the proceeds of criminal activity or related to terrorist financing, it should be required to report promptly to the FIU.
summary: FATF R20 mandates prompt STR reporting to the FIU — the international standard underpinning domestic obligations.

full_text: Countries should establish a financial intelligence unit that serves as a national centre for the receipt and analysis of suspicious transaction reports.
summary: FATF R29 establishes the FIU mandate: receive, analyse, and disseminate financial intelligence to competent authorities.

full_text: Every bank shall comply with all applicable AML/CFT laws and guidelines. Non-compliance may result in enforcement action including suspension or revocation of licence.
summary: Licensed banks must comply with all AML/CFT requirements. Bank of Mauritius can impose enforcement actions for non-compliance.
